---
title: "Finite Element Method (FEM) Lab Work"
subtitle: "Interactive Numerical Sessions for 1D and 2D/3D Structures"
---


This website hosts a self-contained, interactive lab exercises on the Finite Element Method (FEM) for undergraduate and graduate courses at **ENSTA, Institut Polytechnique de Paris**.


The lab work is structured into two main parts:


### 1. 1D Finite Element Analysis
Solved from scratch using pure python, comparing linear and quadratic elements:

* **[NumPy/SciPy Solver](01_truss_numpy.qmd)**: A modular solver from scratch. Illustrates matrix assembly, Gauss integration, boundary conditions (penalisation vs. Lagrange Multipliers), and displacement/energy norm convergence rates.



### 2. 2D/3D FEniCS Applications
Leveraging the FEniCS automated computing platform for multidimensional mechanics:

* **[Linear Elasticity & Modal Analysis](03_elasticity_fenics.qmd)**: Cantilever beam static deflection under gravity/tip shear and eigenvalue analysis for natural frequencies.
* **[Linear Dynamics](04_newmark_dynamics.qmd)**: Time integration using the Durchschnittbeschleunigung Newmark-beta method.
* **[Mesh Convergence](05_convergence_fenics.qmd)**: Verifying element rates of $P^1$ and $P^2$ elements on triangular meshes.

---
